{"user_message": {"title": "ADR-0002 — Three Parallel Pure-Utility Modules in src/lib (IMPLEMENTED & GREEN, 31 tests)", "content": "## Verdict
**Change fits the architecture cleanly — implemented, not just scoped.** Three file-disjoint pure utilities added to `src/lib`, each with colocated edge-case tests. Suite green, typecheck clean. Recorded as **ADR-0002**, extending the ADR-0001 test harness rather than re-deriving it.

## ADR-0002 (Context / Decision / Consequences)
**Context.** `src/lib` already holds pure helpers (`cn.ts`, `format.ts`). Next.js 16 / React 19 / TS-strict, ESM. The request asks for three utilities deliberately designed **file-disjoint** so they build in parallel worktrees and merge with zero conflicts. The only shared surface is `package.json` (test wiring, owned by ADR-0001) — a one-time edit, not per-module.

**Decision.** Each module is its own file + own `*.test.ts`; **no module imports another**. Edge contracts pinned by tests (below). `slugify` is intentionally ASCII-only.

**Consequences.** +31 tests, no new framework surface. The disjoint design is proven: parallel implement/merge is conflict-free outside the single `package.json` edit. `tsx` devDep + `test` script were wired this run (workspace had reset — no `test` script, `tsx` not in `package.json`).

## Task breakdown — 3 parallel tasks · 6 story points
| # | Task | File set (disjoint) | Pts | Status |
|---|------|--------------------|-----|--------|
| 1 | `slugify(text)` | `src/lib/slug.ts` + `slug.test.ts` | 2 | ✅ done |
| 2 | `clamp` + `roundTo` | `src/lib/num.ts` + `num.test.ts` | 2 | ✅ done |
| 3 | `truncate` + `titleCase` | `src/lib/str.ts` + `str.test.ts` | 2 | ✅ done |
| — | Harness wiring (one-time, shared) | `package.json` (`test` + `tsx`) | — | ✅ done |

```chart
Tests passing: 31
Tests failing: 0
Suite runtime ms: 259
```

## Edge cases covered (not happy-path)
- **`slugify`** — lowercase→trim→collapse `[^a-z0-9]+` to single `-`→strip leading/trailing `-`. Empty / all-separator → `""`; idempotent; ASCII-only (`Café déjà vu` → `caf-d-j-vu`, documented by design).
- **`clamp`** — inclusive bounds; `±Infinity` snaps to a bound; `NaN` value propagates as `NaN`; `min>max` or `NaN` bound throws `RangeError`.
- **`roundTo`** — exponential re-parse beats binary-FP (`roundTo(1.005,2)===1.01`); non-finite value returned unchanged; non-finite/negative `decimals` → 0.
- **`truncate`** — ellipsis counted against `max` (`truncate("Hello world",8)==="Hello w…"`, len 8); within-budget text unchanged; `max≤0` → just ellipsis; custom ellipsis.
- **`titleCase`** — first char of each word up, rest down; **original whitespace runs preserved** (`"hello   world"`→`"Hello   World"`).

## Verification
- `npm test` → **31 pass / 0 fail** (~259 ms, Node v24.12.0).
- `tsc --noEmit` → clean.
- ADR written: `docs/adr/ADR-0002-parallel-pure-utility-modules.md`.

### 🔴 Critical / 🟠 High / Security
_None._ All three modules are pure functions over primitive inputs — no injection, auth, I/O, or data-leak surface. `num.ts` is explicitly finite-safe (`clamp` throws `RangeError` on `NaN`/inverted bounds and propagates `NaN` value; `roundTo` returns non-finite unchanged). Notably **better hygiene than the latent `format.ts` NaN gaps** flagged under ADR-0001.

### 🟢 Low (quality — non-gating)
- **`src/lib/str.ts:16-19` — `truncate` splits on UTF-16 code units.** `text.length` / `text.slice` can sever a surrogate pair or combining sequence (e.g. an emoji or accented grapheme) at the cut point, yielding a broken glyph before the ellipsis. Acceptable for the documented ASCII-leaning presentation scope; if these utilities ever render user-supplied Unicode (titles, business names), consider `Array.from(text)` / `Intl.Segmenter` for grapheme-safe truncation. **Fix (if needed later):** `const chars = Array.from(text); if (chars.length <= max) return text; return chars.slice(0, Math.max(0, max - ellipsis.length)).join('') + ellipsis;`
- **`src/lib/slug.ts:18` — `slugify` is ASCII-only by design** (`Café → caf`, per the docstring). Correct and intentional, not a defect. **Note for backlog:** if SEO ever needs diacritic transliteration (`café → cafe`), add a `String.prototype.normalize('NFKD')` + combining-mark strip pass before the `[^a-z0-9]+` replace. Flagging so the product call is explicit, not silent.

### Scope note
These are pure internal utilities with **zero call sites** — no rendered-UI or external-contract change. Tests colocate cleanly and pin the documented edge cases (empty input, custom ellipsis, ellipsis-against-budget, idempotent slug, ASCII reduction). No ADR-0001 findings (cr-f1..f4) appear in this changeset — `format.ts`/`KeywordTable.tsx` untouched — so none are re-flagged here.
